title: Medroxyprogesterone Injection: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
Medroxyprogesterone may cause side effects. Tell your doctor if any of these symptoms are severe or do not go away: 
headache belly pain weight gain nervousness dizziness decreased sex drive swelling, pain, redness or bruising at site of injection Some side effects can be serious. The following side effects are uncommon, but if you experience any of them, call your doctor immediately: 
bulging of the eyeballs, double vision, loss of vision, sudden severe headache warmth or swelling and pain in an extremity severe belly pain rash, hives, itching, blistering skin, shortness or breath or feeling like throat is closing, weakness, extreme tiredness yellowing of skin or whites of eyes, lower abdominal pain, dark urine, pale stools, extreme tiredness, loss of appetite seizures swelling of the legs or feet or ankles, weight gain If you are younger than 35 years old and began to receive medroxyprogesterone injection in the last 4 to 5 years, you may have a slightly increased risk that you will develop breast cancer. Medroxyprogesterone injection may also increase the chance that you will develop a blood clot that moves to your lungs or brain. Talk to your doctor about the risks of using this medication.
Medroxyprogesterone injection may cause meningioma (a type of brain tumor that grows from the meninges, the protective membranes covering the brain and spinal cord). Talk to your doctor
